[doctor] patient mrn : abc123 . date of birth : january 1st , 1960 . patient is a 60 year old , right-handed male , referred today for evaluation of numbness and tingling .
[doctor] hello , mr. cook . how are you doing this morning ?
[patient] hi , dr. martinez . this weekend was a bit rough . i've had this numbness and tingling for a few weeks now .
[doctor] so it's in both of your hands ?
[patient] yes , ever since my car wreck last december when i broke them ... well , once they healed , i mean , i noticed it more , the tingling and the numbness .

[patient] no , i also wear the left sometimes . the right is just worse , but the splint helps . both have really gotten worse over the last month . when i broke them , my right wrist was definitely in worse shape . i've also been having some s- mild stiffness in my neck too . i hit my head during the accident , and they told me it was a contusion .
[doctor] and where would you say the tingling and numbness is ?
[patient] on this side , it goes from here and here up to about here . on the other side , it's from here to here .
[doctor] i see . so on the right side , you're pointing from the middle and ring finger , all the way up the forearm . and on the left , the symptoms extend from the pinky and ring finger . and is there any pain or just tingling and numbness ?
[patient] eh , it's mainly just sore . sometimes they throb , but overall , they feel weaker too , especially when i'm using them . the tingling happens more when they hurt , but when it goes away , the tingling is dull .
[doctor] and when do you feel these symptoms ?
[patient] well , really , i guess , just more so at night , and they come and go throughout the day .
[doctor] okay . and do you experience any loss of sensation in your hands or fingers ?
[patient] like , feeling something ? no , not that i can really think of . though , in general , they may be less sensitive than they should be , but i can still feel . they also gave me some nerve pain medicine , but i have n't been taking it like i should .
[doctor] and are you still on the coreg for hypertension and the lexapro for anxiety ?
[patient] yes , i'm still taking those . i had prediabetes too , but i got it under control by changing my diet .
[doctor] and no history of thyroid issues , correct ?
[patient] nope .
[doctor] all right , thank you , mr. cook . okay , first , we'll go over your review of systems . correct me if i'm wrong in any of this .
[doctor] constitutional , you have fatigue and weight gain ; gi , you have excessive appetite and bloating ; eyes have floaters ; cardiovascular shows shortness of breath and leg swelling ; endocrine , you're always hungry ; skin , you have some nail bed changes , dryness , itching ; musculoskeletal , you have gout and muscle pain . and the remainder of the review of systems is negative .
[patient] yep , that's all correct .
[doctor] all right , we're going to go ahead and do the neurologic examination . let's look at the right hand first . can you feel this ?
[patient] yeah , but a little less on these two fingers .
[doctor] sensation is normal , except pin prick sensation diminished in third and fourth fingers on the right .
[doctor] and how about on the left ?
[patient] um , yeah , but these two a little less .
[doctor] sensation is decreased in the fourth and fifth fingers on the left
[doctor] okay , let's test your reflexes now . reflexes are 1+ and symmetric ; 2+ knees ; toes are downgoing bilaterally .
[doctor] all right , i'm going to tap on your right wrist . do you feel anything ?
[patient] yeah , it feels like a zing up my wrist and something into my elbow too .
[doctor] and how about the left side ?
[patient] yeah , same on that side too .
[doctor] tinel sign positive at both wrists and both elbows . rest of exam is normal .
[doctor] all right , so what i am seeing today is bilateral carpal tunnel syndrome or , potentially , this could also be bilateral cubital tunnel syndrome . however , your symptom presentation suggests car- bilateral carpal syndrome , as the nerve in your wrists are aggravated by your past trauma . this is causing the pain and tingling you were describing to me .
[patient] okay , so how do we make it better ?
[doctor] first , i want to determine exactly where the nerve irritation is coming from . we'll need to schedule you for a nerve test , which is called an emg test . they will measure the responsiveness of your nerves to truly pinpoint where you are having your issues .
[patient] that sounds fine to me .
[doctor] great . after the tests are complete , you can return here for a follow-up visit . if you're still having symptoms , we can consider a referral to our hand clinic , possibly for injections or carpal tunnel release se- surgery . you could also refer ... we could also refer you to an orthopedist for the same treatment .
[patient] sounds good , doc .
[doctor] all right , keep wearing your splints , and i will see you after those studies .
[patient] thank you .

[patient] i am .

[patient] you as well .
[doctor] how can i help you today ?
[patient] well , my primary doctor , dr. scott , he referred me here .
[doctor] okay .
[patient] i've just got really bad acid reflux .
[doctor] okay . can you tell me more about it ?
[patient] yeah . though i mean i ... right now , i take 40 milligrams of omeprazole twice a day . some days , it is fine , but others are just really bad .
[doctor] what do you feel ?
[patient] it feels like burning .
[doctor] in your chest ?
[patient] yes , in my chest and also in my throat .
[doctor] in your throat and chest . all right . any difficulty with swallowing ? feels like food or liquids kinda get stuck ?
[patient] no .
[doctor] okay . that's good . any abdominal pain ?
[patient] no , not really .
[doctor] nausea or vomiting , changes in bowel movements ?
[patient] no .
[doctor] constipation , diarrhea , rectal bleeding ?
[patient] nope .
[doctor] okay . very good . and how long have you had this problem ?
[patient] i've had acid reflux since i was around 12 , but it has gotten worse over the past few years .
[doctor] okay . and how long have you been on the omeprazole ?
[patient] on the way , it is now i think about a year .
[doctor] okay . do you find that it's done anything for you in the past ?
[patient] i mean , i can tell it's helped because some days , i'll be fine , and then some days , i eat something and it feels terrible .
[doctor] are there any things that make your symptoms worse that you can pinpoint , like food choices or stress ?
[patient] sure . probably stress and food choices sometimes .
[doctor] okay . what things bother you ?
[patient] spicy foods , um , so i try not to eat them too often , but sometimes even drinking a soda can cause it .
[doctor] we do know that caffeine and caffeinated beverages will do that , even sodas . um , even just the carbonation can cause that . how about tomato-based products or citrus products ?
[patient] yeah , sometimes , but i still eat them .
[doctor] okay . it's reassuring that you are n't having any difficulty swallowing , though . how's your weight been ? pretty stable ?
[patient] i'd say pretty stable , although i have n't had much of an appetite for the past two months . i have been under a lot of work stress .
[doctor] what do you do for work ?
[patient] i'm a paralegal .
[doctor] okay . and any family history of gi issues ?
[patient] um , my grandmother had esophageal cancer .
[doctor] on your mom or dad's side ?
[patient] mom's side . and my mom had some issues like acid reflux , and she was taking some medication that coats the lining of your stomach .
[doctor] okay . have you ever had an upper endoscopy ?
[patient] i think maybe when i was younger .
[doctor] but not in the last 10 years ?
[patient] no .
[doctor] have you ever tried any medication other than omeprazole ?
[patient] um , at one point i was on omeprazole and ranititine -dine . it did help . but other than that , those are the only medications i have taken .
[doctor] okay . have a seat here , and let me listen to your heart and lungs , and then we can talk about a plan for you .
[patient] okay .

[patient] i always forget about that surgery . i had it when i was a baby . my mom said my intestines were twisted , so i had to have surgery when i was around one month old .
[doctor] hm , gotcha . okay . so let's talk about what you have going on . from what you've told me , it sounds like you've had a several-year history of gerd , which is the medical term for acid reflux . and recently your symptoms have been poorly controlled with the omeprazole , 40 milligrams twice daily . you've been having heartburn despite taking this medication , correct ?
[patient] yes .
[doctor] and you've identified trigger foods like spicy foods , carbonated bev- beverages , citrus , and tomato-based foods , but you have n't cut these foods out of your diet completely yet . so the first thing i'd try is changing your diet because these foods can exacerbate your gerd , even though you're taking the ppi .
[patient] okay , yeah , i could definitely do a better job of avoiding those foods .
[doctor] great . so stress is another trigger that we need to address . high stress can cause worsening of your symptoms .
[patient] yeah , stress and anxiety are definitely big problems for me .
[doctor] so working on that may help with your gerd . additionally , we can consider trying a different ppi since the only one you've tried up to this point is omeprazole . if you're okay with this , we can switch your omeprazole to nexium .
[patient] that's fine with me . i'm definitely open to trying whatever .
[doctor] perfect . i will send a prescription for nexium 40 milligrams twice daily to your pharmacy on file . i'll also recommend that you have a procedure called a egd , or upper endoscopy , to look for potential causes of the gerd . we'd sedate you and then pass a small camera through your mouth , down your throat , through your stomach , and end in the small intestines . we'd look for any ulcers or signs of precancerous or cancerous conditions that we can take biopsies at the same time if we see anything .
[patient] okay . can i schedule that today ?
[doctor] yeah . you can get scheduled at checkout desk . i'll have you follow up with me in the office for the egd so we can discuss the results and talk about how you're doing with the dietary , stress , and medication changes . how does all of that sound ?
[patient] that sounds good .
[doctor] perfect . i will walk you to the checkout desk . be sure to call my office and let me know if anything changes or if you have problems before your egd and follow-up appointment .
[doctor] please insert the procedure paragraph in the plan . i suspect that she likely has gerd with underlying functional etiology as well . reassuringly , she has no unintentional weight loss and denies any dysphagia . if her egd is normal and her symptoms pers- persist despite a trial of nexium , we can consider a trial of protonix and obtain a ph-impedance study . this will allow us to rule out abnormal acid reflux exposure and to decipher between a functional etiology and hypersensitivity .

After the injury, she washed the knee and applied a bandage, and she scheduled this appointment due to ongoing pain. [n0022][n0023][n0024][pn0001_n0022][pn0001_n0023][pn0001_n0024]

PHYSICAL EXAM  
Right knee exam shows a dime-sized, dry abrasion over the medial patella. [n0043][pn0001_n0043]  
There are no scars and no knee deformity noted. [n0044][n0049][pn0001_n0044][pn0001_n0049]  
Right knee range of motion is normal with full extension and full flexion, and the left knee range of motion is also normal with full symmetric flexion. [n0047][n0048][pn0001_n0047][pn0001_n0048]  
There is medial joint line tenderness, while the lateral side is nontender. [n0059][n0042][pn0001_n0059][pn0001_n0042]  
Pes anserine bursa and epicondyle are nontender, and the popliteal space is soft/nontender without posterior knee pain. [n0038][n0039][n0040][n0041][pn0001_n0038][pn0001_n0039][pn0001_n0040][pn0001_n0041]  
Patellar tracking is normal with no crepitus. [n0058][pn0001_n0058]  
Valgus stress testing is interpreted as intact medial and lateral collateral ligaments. [n0051][n0052][n0053][pn0001_n0051][pn0051_n0052][pn0051_n0053]

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN  
Right knee pain after bicycle fall with ongoing swelling and intermittent instability, with clinician concern for a suspected right posterior cruciate ligament injury based on mechanism and exam. [n0003][n0013][n0061][n0008][n0014][pn0001_n0008][pn0003_n0008][pn0001_n0013]  
Because x-rays are normal and internal derangement is still possible (including meniscus or cartilage injury), obtain an MRI of the right knee. [n0070][n0072][n0009][n0074][n0075][pn0001_n0009][pn0002_n0009]  
Begin nonoperative management with a knee brace (including fitting today) and physical therapy to restore knee motion and strength. [n0011][n0080][n0012][n0081][n0071][n0076][n0077][pn0001_n0011][pn0001_n0080][pn0001_n0012][pn0001_n0081]  
For swelling, use ice packs a few times per day and continue ibuprofen up to three times daily for a short time if it is helping, with dose review. [n0013][n0078][n0005][pn0001_n0078][pn0001_n0005][pn0005_n0013]

Follow up for right knee recheck in about 4–6 weeks. [n0082][pn0001_n0082]  
Surgery is discussed as a possible future option that is rarely needed for isolated PCL injury and may be considered if persistent instability continues after bracing/therapy. [n0010][n0073][pn0002_n0010]

Donna is a 37-year-old female with a history of hypertension and diabetes mellitus who presents with headaches and reports her blood pressure has been running high. [n0001][n0004][n0005][pn0001_n0003][pn0001_n0004][pn0001_n0005][n0009]  
She reports headaches for the past three weeks that occur throughout the day, are getting worse, and are about 7/10 in severity. [n0003][n0013]  
She takes Tylenol for the headaches, which helps only briefly and decreases the pain to about 4–5/10 before the headache returns. [n0003][n0007][pn0001_n0007]  
She reports home blood pressure readings including 160/87 and 175/92, with a prior reading of 124/82 noted as pretty normal. [n0010][pn0001_n0010]  
She is currently taking lisinopril for blood pressure but sometimes forgets doses on weekends when busy and checks her blood pressure about once a week. [n0001][n0011][pn0001_n0011][n0009]  
She reports checking blood sugars around 120 (sometimes down to 100), and notes her urination has been normal without increased frequency. [n0006][pn0001_n0006][n0012][pn0001_n0012]  
She has a history of episodic migraines occurring about once a year or every two years and reports she does not really take anything for them. [n0014][pn0001_n0014]  
She denies congestion with the headache, and denies cough and runny nose, though she reports seasonal allergies in the springtime. [n0015][n0016][n0017][n0018][pn0001_n0015][pn0001_n0016][pn0001_n0017][pn0001_n0018]  

PHYSICAL EXAM  
She reports forehead tenderness. [n0019][pn0001_n0019]  
She reports mild cheekbone (maxillary) tenderness and a little neck tenderness. [n0020][n0021][pn0001_n0020][pn0001_n0021]  
She denies frontal sinus tenderness, maxillary sinus tenderness, and lymphadenopathy. [n0022][n0023][n0024][pn0001_n0022][pn0001_n0023][pn0001_n0024]  

RESULTS  
Home blood pressure measurements over the past three weeks have been elevated. [n0009][n0010]  
Reported blood glucose values have been around 120 (sometimes down to 100), noted as a little bit high. [n0006]  
Blood tests are ordered, including hemoglobin A1c. [n0028][n0029][pn0001_n0028][pn0001_n0029]  

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN  
Headache in the setting of elevated blood pressure/hypertension, with plan to treat both by increasing lisinopril. [n0003][n0004][pn0003_n0004][n0026][pn0001_n0026]  
Increase lisinopril from 20 mg once daily to 40 mg once daily. [n0025][n0026][pn0025_n0026][pn0002_n0026]  
She may continue taking Tylenol for headache symptoms. [n0007][n0003]  
Hypertension: continue monitoring with ongoing blood pressure measurements. [n0004][n0027][pn0002_n0027]  
Diabetes mellitus with blood sugars running a little high: obtain hemoglobin A1c and provide diabetic education. [n0005][n0006][n0029][n0030][pn0001_n0029][pn0001_n0030]  
Encouraged to continue walking for exercise. [n0031][pn0002_n0031]  
Plan for follow-up visit after blood tests to review results and ensure blood pressure is under control, and patient is advised to come in and be seen by Doctor Ruth. [n0008][n0002][pn0001_n0008][pn0001_n0002]  
Patient agrees with the plan including increasing lisinopril, continuing to check blood pressure, obtaining blood tests, following up, and continuing walking. [n0001]

note:
HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS  
Michael presents for a follow-up visit after his primary care provider (PCP) was concerned about symptoms and ordered a CT scan. [n0012][n0013][n0004][pn0001_n0012][pn0001_n0013][pn0001_n0004]  
He reports intermittent left-sided back pain in the left lumbar region that is sometimes sharp and sometimes moves. [n0005][pn0001_n0005]  
He reports drinking a lot more water with increased urine output and intermittent nocturia (waking at night to urinate more than normal). [n0018][n0020][n0019][pn0001_n0018][pn0001_n0020][pn0001_n0019]  
He denies being more tired lately and reports his appetite is unchanged. [n0016][n0017]  
He has had recurrent sore throats/recurrent streptococcal tonsillitis over the last five months (record notes four sore throats in that time). [n0006][pn0001_n0006]  
He lives with siblings, reports most siblings are healthy, and notes a brother who smokes at home leading to tobacco smoke exposure in the house. [n0030][n0011][n0032][pn0001_n0030][pn0001_n0011][pn0001_n0032]  
He is in school, reports school is going well with good grades, and has a class right after today’s appointment. [n0023][n0024][n0009][pn0001_n0023][pn0001_n0024][pn0001_n0009]  
He is changing jobs and reports his new job has no health insurance. [n0066][n0069][pn0001_n0066][pn0001_n0069]  
He reports his mother is paying for the tests. [n0062]  

PHYSICAL EXAM  
Throat exam shows no erythema or swelling, and the tonsils are midline and look good. [n0010][n0037][n0038][pn0001_n0010][pn0001_n0038]  
There is no cervical lymphadenopathy on exam. [n0039][n0010][pn0001_n0039]  
Lung exam is clear without wheezes, rales, or rhonchi. [n0041][n0010][pn0001_n0041]  
Cardiac exam shows no murmur, rub, or gallop. [n0042][n0010][pn0001_n0042]  
Abdominal exam is benign with no tenderness, rebound, or guarding, and palpation does not elicit pain. [n0044][n0045][n0010][pn0001_n0044][pn0001_n0045]  
On exam, pressing on the left back area did not hurt. [n0005]  

RESULTS  
CT scan shows findings that are concerning and are discussed as consistent with suspected glomerulonephritis in the setting of recurrent strep episodes. [n0004][n0046][n0006][pn0004_n0046][pn0006_n0046][pn0004_n0006]  

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN  
Suspected glomerulonephritis based on CT findings, with discussion that this could lead to kidney disease including chronic kidney disease. [n0046][n0004][n0047][n0048][pn0004_n0046][pn0046_n0047][pn0046_n0048]  
Order laboratory evaluation including lipid panel, urinalysis, CMP, CBC, vitamin D, TSH, and iron measurement, with blood draw today/as soon as possible (some tests may require fasting per lab). [n0007][n0052][n0053][n0054][n0055][n0056][n0057][n0059][n0008][n0063][pn0001_n0007][pn0001_n0008][pn0007_n0063][pn0008_n0063]  
Refer to ENT for evaluation of recurrent streptococcal tonsillitis and consideration of tonsillectomy. [n0006][n0049][n0051][pn0001_n0049][pn0001_n0051][pn0002_n0051]  
Address household tobacco smoke exposure by planning a conversation with Michael’s brother about smoking at home. [n0032][n0033][n0011][pn0001_n0032][pn0001_n0011]  
No vaccinations are planned today. [n0077]  
Plan follow-up in one week, with phone follow-up if results are normal and in-person follow-up if additional conversations are needed. [n0078][n0079][pn0001_n0078][pn0002_n0079]  
A work note is planned to help him complete blood draws and then go to class. [n0067][n0009][n0008][pn0001_n0067][pn0001_n0009][pn0001_n0008]

note:
HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS  
Billy is a 74-year-old teacher who presents for evaluation of cough and right knee pain after a fall. [n0001][n0003][n0011][pn0001_n0003][pn0001_n0011]  
He reports a productive cough with yellow sputum that started about a week ago and has been getting worse over the last two days, with partial relief from OTC cough/cold medications. [n0003][n0033][pn0001_n0003][pn0001_n0033]  
He reports nasal congestion with postnasal drip and a little sinus pain/pressure. [n0005][n0020][n0021][pn0001_n0005][pn0001_n0020][pn0001_n0021]  
He denies fever and chills and denies shortness of breath, though he feels a little achy at times. [n0004][n0015][n0016][n0017][pn0001_n0004][pn0001_n0015][pn0001_n0016][pn0001_n0017]  
He reports no one else is sick in the household. [n0022][pn0001_n0022]  
This morning, he fell down about four or five stairs while coughing and twisted/injured his right knee with pain mostly on the inside and a slight limp. [n0011][n0028][n0031][pn0001_n0011][pn0001_n0028][pn0001_n0031]  
He reports he is still able to walk and can bend and straighten the knee, and he denies knee instability. [n0029][n0030][n0032][pn0001_n0029][pn0001_n0030][pn0001_n0032]  
His past medical problems include hypertension and diabetes mellitus, and he reports taking lisinopril and metformin. [n0007][n0008][n0009][n0010][pn0001_n0007][pn0001_n0008][pn0001_n0009][pn0001_n0010]  
He reports he is not watching his diet/salt intake and is not monitoring sugar intake or checking blood sugars. [n0025][n0035][n0036][n0078][pn0001_n0025][pn0001_n0035][pn0001_n0036][pn0001_n0078]  

PHYSICAL EXAM  
Vital signs in the office were reassuring, including temperature 98.4, blood pressure 125/62, heart rate in the eighties, and oxygen saturation 97% on room air. [n0046][n0047][n0006][n0048][n0049][pn0001_n0046][pn0001_n0047][pn0001_n0006][pn0001_n0048][pn0001_n0049]  
Facial exam demonstrated bilateral maxillary sinus tenderness. [n0050][n0051][pn0001_n0050][pn0001_n0051]  
Nasal exam showed erythema and edema of the nasal turbinates bilaterally with yellow discharge. [n0053][pn0001_n0053]  
Throat exam showed uvula midline with no erythema, edema, or exudate in the peritonsillar space. [n0054][pn0001_n0054]  
Neck exam demonstrated bilateral cervical lymphadenopathy. [n0055][pn0001_n0055]  
Lung exam revealed coarse rhonchi bilaterally that cleared with cough. [n0056][pn0001_n0056]  
Cardiovascular exam showed regular rate and rhythm with no murmur appreciated. [n0057][pn0001_n0057]  
Right knee exam was notable for pain to palpation and with movement with decreased flexion and extension and ecchymosis over the right medial knee, without evidence of effusion. [n0058][n0059][pn0001_n0058][pn0001_n0059]  

RESULTS  
A lipid panel was ordered. [n0012][pn0001_n0012]  
Hemoglobin A1c was ordered to assess diabetes control and guide potential metformin adjustments. [n0013][n0010][n0077][pn0001_n0013][pn0010_n0013]  
Right knee x-ray (right views) was ordered to evaluate for bony injury after the fall. [n0076][n0028][pn0001_n0076][pn0001_n0028]  
A chest x-ray may be considered if symptoms worsen to help rule out pneumonia. [n0069][n0070][n0071][pn0001_n0069][pn0001_n0070][pn0070_n0071]  

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN  
Cough with upper respiratory infection/viral syndrome and probable mild bronchitis with pulmonary congestion. [n0003][n0060][n0061][n0062][n0063][pn0001_n0060][pn0001_n0061][pn0001_n0062][pn0001_n0063]  
Plan for respiratory symptoms: supportive care and continue OTC medications, and start guaifenesin 600 mg twice daily to help with phlegm. [n0064][n0065][n0066][pn0001_n0064][pn0001_n0065][pn0001_n0066]  
No antibiotics are recommended at this time, and the patient should call if symptoms worsen. [n0067][n0068][pn0001_n0068]  

Right knee injury after fall, assessed as right medial collateral ligament strain with right knee pain. [n0011][n0072][n0059][n0073][pn0001_n0011][pn0001_n0072][pn0001_n0073]  
Plan for knee: start meloxicam 15 mg once daily, obtain right knee x-ray, and refer to physical therapy. [n0074][n0075][n0076][pn0001_n0074][pn0001_n0075][pn0001_n0076]  

Hypertension with normal office blood pressure; continue lisinopril 20 mg daily and obtain a lipid panel. [n0009][n0006][n0007][n0012][pn0001_n0009][pn0001_n0006][pn0001_n0007][pn0001_n0012]  
Diabetes mellitus with poor self-monitoring; order hemoglobin A1c, counsel to monitor sugar intake and report blood sugars through the patient portal, and continue metformin pending results. [n0010][n0036][n0013][n0078][n0079][n0077][pn0001_n0010][pn0001_n0036][pn0001_n0013][pn0002_n0078][pn0001_n0079][pn0001_n0077]  
The patient agrees with the plan and will try to monitor sugar intake and report blood sugars through the patient portal. [n0001]
